name: Visibility of system status
description: The system should always keep users informed about what is going on through appropriate feedback within reasonable time
severity_scale: 0=No feedback at all,1=Delayed or unclear feedback,2=Feedback exists but inconsistent,3=Minor gaps in feedback,4=Comprehensive real-time feedback
example_violation: Form submits with no loading indicator or success message — user clicks Save and nothing happens
example_fix: Add spinner on button during submission and show success toast with checkmark after completion

name: Match between system and real world
description: The system should speak the user's language with words and concepts familiar to the user rather than system-oriented terms
severity_scale: 0=Jargon-filled interface,1=Many unfamiliar terms,2=Some developer terms leak through,3=Mostly user-friendly with minor issues,4=Natural language throughout
example_violation: Error message shows "ERR_NULL_POINTER: Object reference not set" or dropdown shows database enum values like "ACTIVE_STATUS_1"
example_fix: Show "Something went wrong. Please try again." and use human-readable labels like "Active" instead of enum codes

name: User control and freedom
description: Users often choose system functions by mistake and need a clearly marked emergency exit to leave the unwanted state without going through an extended dialogue
severity_scale: 0=No undo or escape possible,1=Limited exit options,2=Some actions irreversible,3=Most actions reversible with minor gaps,4=Full undo/redo and clear exits
example_violation: Delete button removes record immediately with no confirmation or undo — data is permanently lost
example_fix: Add confirmation dialog "Delete this item? This cannot be undone." with Cancel/Delete buttons and optional 5-second undo toast

name: Consistency and standards
description: Users should not have to wonder whether different words or situations or actions mean the same thing — follow platform conventions
severity_scale: 0=No consistent patterns,1=Major inconsistencies,2=Several inconsistent elements,3=Mostly consistent with minor deviations,4=Fully consistent patterns
example_violation: Save button appears on the left in one form but on the right in another; some modals close with X and others with Cancel
example_fix: Establish and document a pattern: primary action always on the right and all modals have both X button and Cancel

name: Error prevention
description: Even better than good error messages is a careful design which prevents a problem from occurring in the first place
severity_scale: 0=No error prevention,1=Minimal prevention,2=Some input constraints,3=Good prevention with gaps,4=Comprehensive prevention
example_violation: Date picker allows selecting end date before start date or email field accepts any text without validation
example_fix: Disable dates before start date in the end-date picker and validate email format on blur with inline feedback

name: Recognition rather than recall
description: Minimize the user's memory load by making objects and actions visible — the user should not have to remember information from one part of the dialogue to another
severity_scale: 0=Heavy reliance on memory,1=Many recall requirements,2=Some hidden information,3=Mostly visible with minor gaps,4=Everything visible in context
example_violation: Employee selection shows only ID numbers requiring users to memorize which ID belongs to which person
example_fix: Show employee name with avatar and department in the dropdown with search/filter capability

name: Flexibility and efficiency of use
description: Accelerators — unseen by the novice user — may often speed up the interaction for the expert user such that the system can cater to both inexperienced and experienced users
severity_scale: 0=No shortcuts or efficiency features,1=Very limited efficiency options,2=Some shortcuts available,3=Good shortcuts with minor gaps,4=Comprehensive accelerators for experts
example_violation: Every action requires clicking through 3 menus — no keyboard shortcuts or bulk operations available
example_fix: Add Ctrl+S to save and Ctrl+Z to undo; provide bulk select with checkboxes and batch actions toolbar

name: Aesthetic and minimalist design
description: Dialogues should not contain information which is irrelevant or rarely needed — every extra unit of information competes with relevant units and diminishes their relative visibility
severity_scale: 0=Overwhelming clutter,1=Significant noise,2=Some unnecessary elements,3=Mostly clean with minor clutter,4=Every element earns its space
example_violation: Dashboard shows 15 metrics in equal-sized cards with no hierarchy — user cannot identify what matters most
example_fix: Prioritize 3-4 key metrics prominently and group secondary metrics in a collapsible section or separate tab

name: Help users recognize and recover from errors
description: Error messages should be expressed in plain language indicating the problem and constructively suggest a solution
severity_scale: 0=No error handling or cryptic errors,1=Generic error messages,2=Some helpful errors,3=Good error messages with minor gaps,4=All errors are clear with recovery steps
example_violation: Toast shows "Error" and disappears after 2 seconds — user has no idea what went wrong or how to fix it
example_fix: Show inline error near the field: "Email already registered. Try signing in instead." with a link to the sign-in page

name: Help and documentation
description: Even though it is better if the system can be used without documentation it may be necessary to provide help and documentation — any such information should be easy to search and focused on the user's task
severity_scale: 0=No help available,1=Minimal documentation,2=Help exists but hard to find,3=Good help with minor gaps,4=Contextual help throughout
example_violation: Complex reporting feature has no tooltips or explanation — users guess what each filter and option does
example_fix: Add (?) tooltip icons next to complex fields with brief explanations and link to a focused help article
